title: The Defector
premise: Dr. Elena Vasquez, a high-level weapons scientist from the rival nation of Valdoria, has walked into your embassy and requested asylum, carrying a locked briefcase she claims contains classified missile guidance data. Half of your agency is convinced it's a trap, but the opportunity is too valuable to ignore.
setting: Your nation's embassy in the neutral city of Geneva. It's a secure compound, but Valdorian agents are known to operate in the area. The defector is currently in a safe room, and you have a limited window to decide whether to accept her and extract her, or turn her away before Valdoria forces a confrontation.
goal: Determine if Dr. Vasquez is a genuine defector or a plant, and extract her (or the data) safely within 24 hours. Success: secure her and the briefcase, or at least the intelligence. Failure: she's taken back by Valdoria, the briefcase is lost, or she's revealed as a double agent compromising your network.
gm_notes: Dr. Vasquez's defection is real, but she has been turned into an unwitting pawn: the briefcase has a tracking device, and Valdorian operatives are closing in. Her handler, 'Echo', is a Valdorian agent embedded in your embassy, feeding false intel to sow doubt. Pacing: introduce a time pressure (e.g., an ultimatum from Valdoria) and complications like a mole among the NPCs. Secrets: the briefcase data is incomplete and needs a key from Vasquez; the real prize is her knowledge of a new stealth radar.

roles.name: Field Officer
roles.description: The embassy's intelligence liaison, responsible for assessing the defector and managing the extraction.
roles.private_info: You have a personal history with Dr. Vasquez—she helped you escape a Valdorian prison two years ago. You believe she's genuine, but that memory could be a manipulation.

roles.name: Security Chief
roles.description: Head of embassy security, paranoid and suspicious. You suspect traps everywhere.
roles.private_info: You intercepted a comms fragment indicating a Valdorian agent codenamed 'Echo' is active in the embassy. You haven't shared this yet.

roles.name: Technical Analyst
roles.description: Expert in weapons systems, assigned to verify the briefcase's data.
roles.private_info: You recognized a coding pattern in the briefcase's lock that matches a known Valdorian disinformation device. You're unsure if that makes the defector fake or if it's a double bluff.

npcs.name: Dr. Elena Vasquez
npcs.description: Calm, collected weapons scientist. She claims to have defected due to moral objections to new Valdorian weapons.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Genuine defector but is unaware that the briefcase has a bug. She wants to defect and protect her family, who are still in Valdoria.

npcs.name: Ambassador Sterling
npcs.description: The embassy's career diplomat, cautious and by-the-book. He wants to avoid an international incident.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He's under pressure from home to avoid provoking Valdoria. He may order you to reject the defector if things get too hot.

npcs.name: Tomas Beck
npcs.description: A junior analyst at the embassy, eager and helpful, always first to volunteer for extra work.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He is 'Echo', the Valdorian agent embedded in the embassy. His mission: ensure the defector is either killed or retrieved by Valdoria. He feeds doubts and misdirection and will subtly sabotage any extraction plan.
